_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for pages/support/discord.html. Drives a single-card / sections-within layout via render_discord.py.
_meta.notes: Join link placeholder 'YOUR-INVITE-LINK' preserved from original; replace with the real invite URL when the server is ready.

card.subBanner: Connect With Classmates • Get Help • Build Community

overview.label: Overview
overview.body_html: This course has a dedicated Discord server for collaboration, peer support, and community. Participation is entirely optional — no course content or grading depends on it. Before joining, read and understand the terms on this page.

rows.right: Anything that belongs in an email

warningCallout.title: Not a UT San Antonio System
warningCallout.body_html:
This Discord server is not operated, managed, or monitored by UT San Antonio. It is a third-party platform subject to Discord’s own Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, not UT San Antonio’s data protection policies.

UT San Antonio cannot guarantee the security, privacy, or availability of any content shared on this server. Your participation is entirely voluntary and at your own discretion.

implications.heading: What This Means for You
items_html: Use a nickname or first name only — do not use your full legal name on a third-party platform
Never share your student ID, phone number, address, or other personally identifiable information
Do not post screenshots of grades, Canvas messages, or instructor feedback
Treat this space as a professional community, not an extension of Canvas

rules.heading: Rule 1 — Protect Your Privacy
rules.body_html: Use a nickname or first name only. Do not share your student ID, full name, phone number, address, or other personally identifiable information in any channel.

rules.heading: Rule 2 — Keep Academic Records Off Discord
rules.body_html: Do not post screenshots of grades, Canvas notifications, or instructor feedback. These conversations happen in email or office hours, not in a public server.

rules.heading: Rule 3 — Ask in Channels, Not DMs
rules.body_html: Questions asked publicly help everyone. If you have a question, others probably do too. Posting in your module’s #module-N-help channel means your classmates can benefit from the answer as well.

rules.heading: Rule 4 — Be Respectful. Zero Tolerance for Toxicity.
rules.body_html:
This is a professional learning community. Treat your instructor, classmates, and alumni with respect at all times.

  • No personal attacks, insults, harassment, or bullying
  • No passive-aggressive comments or hostile tone
  • No public venting about grades or deadlines — take it to email
  • Disagreements happen; handle them like a professional or walk away

rules.heading: Rule 5 — No Academic Integrity Violations
rules.body_html: Helping each other learn is encouraged. Sharing quiz answers, copying lab work, or completing assignments for others is not. When in doubt, help someone understand the concept — don’t hand them the answer.

enforcementCallout.title: Enforcement
enforcementCallout.body_html: Minor rule violations may receive a warning. Toxic, hostile, or disrespectful behavior results in immediate permanent removal without warning. Harassment, threats, or academic integrity violations will also be reported to UT San Antonio Student Conduct.

serverChannels.label: Server Channels
serverChannels.intro_html: The server is organized into channels so conversations stay focused and easy to search. Here’s where everything lives:

channels.name: #announcements
channels.desc: Read-only. Important course updates, reminders, and links from the instructor. Check this first if something seems off.

channels.name: #course-docs
channels.desc: Pinned reference docs and links to course resources.

channels.name: #general-discussion
channels.desc: Off-topic chat, wins, interesting finds, and community conversation.

channels.name: #course-questions
channels.desc: General course questions — syllabus, deadlines, policy, anything not module-specific.

channels.name: #module-1-help — #module-5-help
channels.desc: One channel per module. Post code questions and debugging help in the channel that matches the module where the problem lives. Use code blocks (triple backticks) when pasting errors or snippets.

channels.name: #technical-questions
channels.desc: Python install, VS Code setup, environment issues — tooling questions that aren’t about your code itself.

channels.name: #tools-and-resources
channels.desc: Useful links, tutorials, references, and tools shared by students and the instructor.

tipCallout.title: Formatting Text in Discord
tipCallout.body_html:
Discord supports Markdown for formatting messages — bold, italic, code blocks, lists, headers, and more.

For code or error messages, wrap them in triple backticks so Discord renders them as a code block. Type ```, paste your content, then ``` again. This makes your question much easier to read and answer.

Want to know more? The Markdown Guide walks through the full syntax.

joinTheServer.label: Join the Server
joinTheServer.subtitle_html: By clicking, you acknowledge the following
joinTheServer.intro_html: Before clicking the join link, confirm that you understand and agree to the following:
agreement_items_html: You have read and understand the terms on this page
Participation is voluntary and not required for any grade
The server is not a UT San Antonio system and is not subject to university data protections
You are responsible for protecting your own privacy
You agree to maintain a respectful, professional tone at all times
You understand that toxic behavior results in immediate permanent removal
You agree to follow all server rules or accept removal
joinTheServer.welcome_html: After joining, introduce yourself in #introductions — we’re glad to have you!
